questions.description: Critically examine current Irish Landlord and Tenant Law having regard to the following: (a) The influence that Deasy's…
questions.text: Critically examine current Irish Landlord and Tenant Law having regard to the following: (a) The influence that Deasy's Act 1860 has had in the past and continues to have on the question of landlord and tenant law in Ireland [AND]{.underline} (b) The role that the Residential Tenancies Act 2004 currently plays and will play in the future.

questions.description: In March 2010, Anita, who was an elderly lady, discovered that she had a… Advise Brian and Dylan as to the terms and…
questions.text: In March 2010, Anita, who was an elderly lady, discovered that she had a lump in her leg. Upon further investigation at the hospital, it was decided that the lump was cancerous, should be removed as soon as possible and further treatment would be required. Before Anita went into hospital for treatment, she decided that she had better draw up a will making provision for her husband, Brian. She didn't feel there was any need to include her son, Dylan as she believed that when Brian died, Dylan would ultimately inherit everything. Anita asked her solicitor, Liz, if she could draw up the will according to her instructions and drop it by her house for signature the week before the operation was due to take place. However, a few days later Anita won €50,000 on a scratch card which she collected. She decided that it would be important to include this in her will also. When Liz called to the house with the will, Anita signed the will in the presence of Liz and Dylan. Brian was walking into the room next door when Anita was initialling the will. "I think you are supposed to watch me doing this love!" Anita called out to him. "I can see you signing it darling" he replied as he had his back turned. After Liz left the house, Anita suddenly remembered the money that she had won on the scratch card and added it to the bottom of the will, indicating that it was to go to Dylan. She thought that she should get her signature witnessed so asked both Dylan and his friend (who happened to be there at the time) to sign the will. Later that evening, Brian came in and sat by the fire. It was nearly gone out so he threw some old newspaper into the fire to try and keep it going. The will which was stuck in the newspaper was thrown into the fire with the newspaper and destroyed. That night, Anita died in her sleep. Dylan claims that he is entitled to the €50,000 that his mother intended to leave to him but Brian disagrees. Advise Brian and Dylan as to the terms and validity of the will.

questions.description: In relation to the legal right share of a spouse, assess the impact that section 111 of the Succession Act 1965 has had…
questions.text: In relation to the legal right share of a spouse, assess the impact that section 111 of the Succession Act 1965 has had on restricting a testator's testamentary freedom, supporting your answer with relevant authority and analysis.

questions.description: His estate consisted of a substantial farm in North Cork. Advise the parties, supporting your answer with relevant…
questions.text: Tom passed away in April 2010. His estate consisted of a substantial farm in North Cork. In his will, Tom left his estate "...to my three children: Tod, Fran and Jim". Soon after the death of their father, Tod has a serious fight with Fran and Jim over what was to be done with their father's estate. Tod, refusing to leave the farm idle, took possession of the farm and has worked it ever since. As a result of the fight, Tod refused to talk to Fran or Jim or allow them onto the property. He was of the opinion that he was the only one who ever cared about their father in recent years and so he should have been left the farm in its entirety in any case. In the last year, both Fran and Jim have lost their jobs and want to sell their respective shares in the property to Tod. However, they cannot reach agreement with Tod over the purchase price as Tod feels it should be sold at a lower price. A local property developer has expressed an interest in the land and Fran and Jim think that it is a good idea to sell the farm. However, Tod opposes the sale. Advise the parties, supporting your answer with relevant authority.

questions.description: In the Irish case of Dunne v Irish Rail and Anor [2007] IEHC 314, Clarke J stated that '...the nature of the possession…
questions.text: In the Irish case of Dunne v Irish Rail and Anor [2007] IEHC 314, Clarke J stated that '...the nature of the possession which must be established is one which must be objectively viewed by reference to the lands concerned and the type of use which one might reasonably expect a typical owner to put those lands to'. Critically analyse this statement with reference to relevant case law.

questions.description: After a night out, John decided to take a short cut home through a meadow which was beside his house.
questions.text: After a night out, John decided to take a short cut home through a meadow which was beside his house. While trespassing on this property, he tripped over something which was half buried in the soil. Upon further investigation, he realised that he had discovered what appeared to be a very valuable silver chalice. Janet, who had taken out a ninety-nine year lease on the meadow the previous year, claimed that she should be entitled to the find. As word got around the local village, Janet's landlord, Simon, heard about the find and claimed that since he owned the field, he should be entitled to the chalice. However, John now claims that since he was the one to discover the chalice, he should be entitled to keep it. Discuss.

questions.description: Critically analyse the legislative protection afforded to spouses with regard to family property in Ireland.
questions.text: Critically analyse the legislative protection afforded to spouses with regard to family property in Ireland. Support your answer with relevant legal authority.

questions.description: Fred has issued a new lease to George of cellars which lie below and adjacent to property retained by him.
questions.text: Fred has issued a new lease to George of cellars which lie below and adjacent to property retained by him. The lease agreement specifically states that the cellars will be used for a new tapas bar. However, upon inspection of these cellars, the local Health Authority has stated that the ventilation system which is in place is wholly inadequate and a new ventilation system will have to be installed. George has acted accordingly and hired a company to install the new system which involves fixing new piping to the property that has been retained by the landlord. Fred immediately questions George's right to do this and now seeks your advice. Discuss with reference to relevant case law.

questions.description: Critically evaluate the legislative protection afforded spouses and same-sex couples in relation to family property in…
questions.text: Critically evaluate the legislative protection afforded spouses and same-sex couples in relation to family property in Ireland. Support your answer with reference to appropriate authority.

questions.description: In 2007, four close college friends, David, Paul, Mathew and Alex, bought… Advise the parties.
questions.text: In 2007, four close college friends, David, Paul, Mathew and Alex, bought a duplex apartment together. Title to the apartment was not registered in the Land Registry. They each paid an equal share of the purchase price. Shortly afterwards, David lost his job and decided to emigrate and sold his interest to Paul. In 2008, Paul unexpectedly became a father and decided, without legal advice, that it was a good time to make a will whereby he would leave his entire share in the property to his son. In 2009, Mathew moved out and rented a room in a house a few doors away because he was finding it very difficult to study for his final year exams in the apartment due to elevated noise levels. This caused an argument between Alex and Paul which resulted in Paul moving all of Alex's possessions, including his plasma screen television and his x-box, from the living room and locking the living room door. On hearing this, Alex's girlfriend suggested that he live with her in her house and so Alex then moved out. Mathew and Alex believe that Paul should pay them rent. Alternatively, Mathew suggests that the apartment should be sold. Alex is uncertain as to whether the apartment should be sold because its market value has fallen a great deal over the past couple of years due to the recession. Paul says that he is not willing to pay them rent and that they are welcome to stay in the apartment. He objects to selling the apartment because he will not be able to find another home in the vicinity that is suitable for him given its close proximity to his place of work. He is also concerned as to whether his would be adequately provided if he were to does unexpectedly. Advise the parties.

questions.description: The succession Act 1965 "...is based on the idea that a testator owes a duty to leave part of his estate to his widow…
questions.text: The succession Act 1965 "...is based on the idea that a testator owes a duty to leave part of his estate to his widow (the legal right) and to make proper provision for his children in accordance with his means. It is not based on a duty to provide maintenance for his widow nor is it limited in its application to children who were dependent on him". Per Kenny J, FM v. TAM (1972) 106 ILTR 82, 86. Critically discuss this statement with reference to case law.

questions.description: Janet is the daughter of Mildred who recently passed away after a life long battle with cancer.
questions.text: Janet is the daughter of Mildred who recently passed away after a life long battle with cancer. When Mildred was 55 years old, Janet gave up her job in a local solicitors firm to move in and care for her mother. She continued to do this until Mildred died when she was 87 years of age. Mildred was disabled as a result of her illness and there was an informal agreement that Mildred's illness benefit would be paid to Janet to meet the needs of them both. Janet also received carer's allowance for looking after her mother. All the bills were paid out of Mildred's savings. Mildred said on many occasion to Janet "I really don't know how I would get on without you taking care of me. When I am gone, you will have a secure home". Janet assumed this meant Mildred would leave her the property. However, Mildred died intestate and has left five children. Under the rules of intestacy, the house will be divided amongst them all equally. Janet seeks your advice on whether she has any rights in relation to the property which are binding on her siblings? Would your answer be any different if the other children had signed a contract 10 years ago clearly stating that when Mildred died, that they would allow Janet to continue living in the house?

questions.description: The Residential Tenancies Act 2004 contains a number of significant reforms for the private rented sector in Ireland,…
questions.text: The Residential Tenancies Act 2004 contains a number of significant reforms for the private rented sector in Ireland, in particular, in the area of security of tenure and tenancy termination procedures. Consider whether the Residential Tenancies Act 2004 has succeeded in its aim of providing a clear legislative framework for tenancy obligations for landlords and tenants in Ireland.

questions.description: One Friday evening, while out exploring with his dog on his father's land,… Advise the parties as to who is entitled to…
questions.text: One Friday evening, while out exploring with his dog on his father's land, 12 year old Simon discovered a hidden entrance to a cave which was accessible from both his father's land and that of his neighbour, Mr. Burns. Simon and his dog walked over a cardboard sign on the ground which read "Keep Out- No Trespassing". However, Simon and the dog carried on regardless and ventured further into the cave. After a few minutes, Simon discovered a gold brooch and some other pieces of old fashioned jewellery which were half-buried in the ground. He wiped off the mud and saw that on the back of it there was an engraving "All my love to my dearest T, always and forever". Simon dropped the brooch and the other artefacts into his pocket of his jacket. Just then, Tim, who had sold Simon's father the land ten years previously, appeared in the cave with Simon's father and claimed that he should have the find as it belonged to him. Advise the parties as to who is entitled to the brooch.

questions.description: Recent case law in relation to adverse possession has suggested a lesser role for the intentions and future plans of…
questions.text: Recent case law in relation to adverse possession has suggested a lesser role for the intentions and future plans of the legal owner and has placed greater emphasis on the animus possidendi of the squatter. Discuss this statement, supporting your answer with relevant legal authority.

questions.description: Lesley did last month from severe heart attack, leaving a net estate worth…
questions.text: Lesley did last month from severe heart attack, leaving a net estate worth 500,000. In 2005, Lesley had made a will. The only bequest in the will was one of "50,000 to my best friend, Jimmy Jones". However, in the will, the figure 40,000 had been crossed out and the figure "100,000" written above it in black biro (the rest of the will had been typewritten). Lesley's solicitor now admits that there was a mistake in the drafting of the will. The week after Lesley had first given instructions as to the terms of the will, the solicitor's secretary had received a phone call from Lesley saying that he felt he should leave more to Jimmy Jones to 100,000. Unfortunately, the secretary, who was leaving to go on holidays that day, totally forgot to pass the message on to the solicitor. The solicitor is not sure how the figure of 100,000 came to appear on the will but believes that it is in the testator's handwriting. One of the witnesses to the will, Delilah, was cohabiting with Jimmy when the will was executed and the couple were married to months later. Lesley was a bachelor and had no children. Lesley's parents both died twenty years ago. However, he was survived by one of his sisters, Julie, with whom he had lost touch. His other twin sisters, Jackie and Ellie, died ten years ago. At the time of Lesley's death, Jackie was survived by her children, Jack and Jill, and Ellie was survived by her two grandchildren, Tom and Jerry. Advise as to proper distribution of Lesley's estate.
